reasoning: Q3 is a critical bottleneck (1272ms, 13x slower than DuckDB, 75% of total execution time). Root cause: single-threaded table scans and joins on 64-core machine, wasting 98% of CPU. Priorities 1-3 target Q3 parallelization + hash table improvements with 8-10x combined speedup potential and low-medium risk. No re-ingestion required.
focus_areas: Replace std::unordered_map with Swiss tables (all queries)
strategy_notes: Focus exclusively on Q3 parallelization first (priorities 1-2) to unlock 8-9x speedup. Then apply Swiss tables (priority 3) for additional 1.5-2x across all queries. Use morsel-driven parallelism with chunk size ~937K rows for 60M lineitem scan (60M/64 cores). Target: Q3 from 1272ms → ~100-150ms (approaching DuckDB's 99ms).
notes: These three optimizations are complementary and low-medium risk. Parallel scans (priority 1) is LOW risk and well-established. Parallel join probes (priority 2) is MEDIUM risk but we have rollback capability. Swiss tables (priority 3) is LOW risk drop-in replacement. Avoid priorities 4-7 this iteration (SIMD, mmap, zone maps) to keep changes focused and diagnosable. Save storage redesign (zone maps) for iteration 3 if needed.
